description: banana v2 — agent-first inversion of the continuity kit. The real user is the agents: wiring blocks become thin stable bootstrap pointers at a living canon; a sync command propagates canon updates; init/project gain owner inference and non-TTY fail-fast; canon moves to a kit-owned <home>/.agents/canon/ dir. Authoritative done-whens: .agents/goal/packet.md — read Goal + ASSUMED lines + the matching task before any story. Packet tasks T3/T3-R (qualitative REVIEW) and T10 (ship) are adjudicated OUTSIDE this loop — never attempt them. Do not touch: any path outside this repo except OS temp dirs; no npm publish; no network calls. Existing behavioral contracts (brief include/exclude, doctor v1 audits, adapter + fence contracts): docs/DESIGN.md — v2 changes extend these, they do not silently break them.

userStories.title: Canon rev 1.2 (bootstrap + upstream/sync sections, version markers)
userStories.description: As an agent landing in a workspace with zero prior context, I want the canon to carry an agent bootstrap section, so that I can self-initialize any workspace (git repo or non-code topic dir) from the canon alone. Extend canon/ per packet T1: bootstrap section (which files to create, entry envelope, session ritual), upstream/sync section (canon dir is kit-owned and overwritable by sync; STATE.md, logs, logbooks are user-owned and never overwritten by the kit), an HTML-comment version marker line containing 1.2 in each canon file, and the changes-from-v1 section extended with the v1.2 additions.
acceptanceCriteria: node --test test/canon.test.mjs exits 0
The test asserts the bootstrap section heading, the upstream/sync section heading, a version marker containing '1.2' in each canon file, and workspace/topic (non-git) language
The test asserts all prior v1.1 required strings are still present
The test asserts zero occurrences of 'Ahimsa', 'VICTUS', 'hermes.exe', or absolute C:/ paths across canon/
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wiring templates v2 (thin bootstrap-pointer blocks, v2 fences)
userStories.description: As an agent reading a wired instruction file, I want the fenced block to be a thin stable bootstrap pointer, so that protocol updates propagate via the canon instead of re-wiring. Rewrite templates/wiring/claude-code.md, agents-md.md, portable-directive.md per packet T2: agent identity (tag), pointer to <home>/.agents/canon/ as protocol authority, the canonical invocation string 'npx --yes github:Q9-Ahimsa/banana', the self-setup instruction (initialize any workspace lacking continuity files per the canon bootstrap section), and the session ritual one-liner. Bump fence markers to v2 ('<!-- banana:begin v2 -->'). Protocol rules live behind the pointer, not in the block.
acceptanceCriteria: node --test test/templates.test.mjs exits 0
The test asserts every wiring template contains both v2 fence markers, the canon dir pointer, the npx invocation string, and a self-setup instruction
The test asserts each wiring block body is 30 lines or fewer
The test asserts only __OWNER__/__AGENT_TAG__ tokens appear and there is zero double-brace residue in templates/
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: init v2 (owner inference, non-TTY fail-fast, canon dir install)
userStories.description: As a colleague running one command once, I want init to infer my identity and never hang headless, so that setup is a single npx call in any environment. Per packet T4: owner resolution order --owner flag, then git config --get user.name, then interactive prompt only when stdin is a TTY (TTY state and the git-config lookup injectable for tests); non-TTY with no inferable owner exits 1 printing the exact non-interactive invocation naming --owner and --yes. init installs canon files into <home>/.agents/canon/ (created or refreshed to the bundled canon), keeps <home>/.agents/STATE.md create-if-missing, wires adapters with the v2 blocks; hermes compose/deliver and paste-instruction paths keep their current behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: node --test test/init.e2e.test.mjs exits 0
Sandbox-HOME e2e: zero-flag init succeeds when a git-config owner stub is injected
Non-TTY init with no owner exits 1 and the output names --owner and --yes
Canon dir populated with version-marked files; STATE.md preserved when pre-existing; second run byte-identical tree
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: sync command (canon refresh + fence re-apply)
userStories.description: As a machine wired last month, I want one command that pulls the current canon and re-fences stale blocks, so that whatever the upstream updates propagates everywhere. Per packet T5: sync copies the kit's bundled canon over <home>/.agents/canon/, re-applies the fenced block to every already-wired harness file whose block version is older than current, reports each change, exits 0; it never touches user-owned surfaces (STATE.md, session logs, logbooks). No network code — npx fetching the latest kit IS the transport.
acceptanceCriteria: node --test test/sync.test.mjs exits 0
Sandbox with stale canon + a v1 fence block: after sync the canon dir is byte-equal to the kit's bundled canon and the fence block reads v2 with user content outside the fence byte-identical
STATE.md untouched by sync
A second sync run reports no changes and the tree is byte-identical
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: project v2 (topic-grain: non-git proceeds, shared owner inference)
userStories.description: As an agent initializing a workspace that may not be a code project, I want project to proceed in a non-git dir, so that topics get continuity too. Per packet T6: non-git dir proceeds by default printing a not-version-controlled note; the --force flag is removed; owner resolution shares init's inference order and non-TTY fail-fast; created files and the AGENTS.md fence content follow the v2 templates.
acceptanceCriteria: node --test test/project.e2e.test.mjs exits 0
Non-git temp dir: exit 0, files created, note present in output; git temp repo: exit 0, note absent
Non-TTY with no inferable owner exits 1 naming the flags
Re-run idempotent
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: brief discovery (no-arg listing, unknown-slug listing)
userStories.description: As an agent starting a session without knowing the active slugs, I want brief with no argument to list them, so that slug discovery needs no grep. Per packet T7: no feature arg prints the active-slug listing (slug + date of its latest entry) and exits 0; an unknown slug prints the same listing to stderr and exits 1; the compiled-brief contract for a known slug is unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: node --test test/brief.test.mjs exits 0
Fixture logs: no-arg run lists both fixture slugs with dates and exits 0
Unknown-slug run exits 1 and stderr carries the listing
All prior include/exclude assertions still pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: doctor v2 (stale-canon + stale-fence audits naming sync)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want doctor to detect a stale canon or stale fence blocks, so that drift from upstream is caught mechanically. Per packet T8: two new audits — canon dir missing or any canon file's version marker older than the kit's bundled canon; any wired fence block at a version older than current — each finding names sync as the remediation. The existing four audits and exit semantics are unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: node --test test/doctor.test.mjs exits 0
Stale-canon and stale-fence fixtures each flagged with exit 1 and the finding text contains 'sync'
Current-canon fixture adds no finding
The four v1 audit assertions still pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: README inversion + version 0.2.0
userStories.description: As a colleague seeing banana for the first time, I want one screen that says run this and your agents handle the rest, so that adoption needs no protocol reading. Per packet T9: package.json to 0.2.0; README human section is one screen (npx one-liner, agents-handle-the-rest framing, the sync update story), everything below addressed to agents (five-command reference including sync, canon pointer, bootstrap model); the four-command human tour and human-facing daily-ritual sections are removed.
acceptanceCriteria: node --test test/readme.test.mjs exits 0
README contains the npx one-liner, all five command names, and the canon dir path
The human section (content above the first agent-addressed heading) is 40 lines or fewer
node bin/banana.mjs --version prints 0.2.0
Typecheck passes
